topics.headline: California May Revision — what changes for independent study charters and intervention funding
topics.why_matters: The May Revise sets the Prop 98 guarantee, LCFF base/supplemental rates, and any new/sunsetting categorical programs for the school year A+ is currently selling into. Every Tier A partner — especially the Sacramento cluster (Visions, Rio Valley, Heritage, Horizon) and the Sonoma cluster (Ocean Grove, Heartwood) — is rebuilding LCAPs against these numbers right now. A+ is one of the few intervention vendors that can read a May Revise and translate it into a school-level funding action.
topics.angle: "Three line items in the May Revise that change how charters should plan intervention spending for 2026-27" — concrete, school-facing, not a hot take.
topics.roman_take: The May Revise is a referendum on whether California still believes academic recovery is a real emergency — and the answer this year is "sort of." Schools that wait for the state to fund urgency will keep waiting.
topics.danielle_take: Walk through exactly which line items in the May Revise map to Title III Immigrant, Equity Multiplier, and LCFF supplemental — and which ones charter directors should be moving on this month, not in August. ---

topics.headline: Independent study charter oversight and the cost of being "the easy school to cut"
topics.category: C (Tier A partner cluster) with B (problem story) overlay
topics.why_matters: Eight of A+'s 23 Tier A partners are independent study / NCB charters operating under AB 130 + AB 1316 oversight regimes. Every legislative session brings new pressure on this model. When oversight tightens, intervention documentation is the first thing auditors look at — and "we contract with A+" is a cleaner audit answer than "we built it in-house." A+ is uniquely positioned to comment because we serve this exact charter type at scale.
topics.angle: Frame A+ as the intervention partner that makes independent study charters *more* auditable, not less. Lead with: "When the oversight pendulum swings, charters with documented Tier 2/3 intervention plans sleep better." Include the count of NCB partners we serve without naming them.
topics.roman_take: The conviction angle is that independent study charters are doing the *harder* work of personalization and getting punished for it — and that outsourced, ESSA-tiered intervention is a moat against bad-faith oversight, not a luxury.
topics.danielle_take: The implementation angle is the documentation reality — what intervention paperwork actually survives a charter audit, and how A+'s reporting cadence (session logs, growth data, Title III/I alignment) maps to what auditors ask for.

topics.headline: The on-demand tutoring model is quietly failing districts — and procurement teams are noticing
topics.why_matters: Every major on-demand/async tutoring vendor that raised at scale during ESSER is now visibly struggling to renew. The reason is structural, not operational: async tutoring fails the NSSA "high-dosage" definition (3+ sessions/week, same tutor, embedded in school day). A+'s scheduled, recurring, named-tutor model is the affirmative case study for what these vendors can't deliver. This is the rare moment when A+ can comment on a competitor category without naming names and still have the audience know exactly what's meant.
topics.angle: "What districts learned the hard way about 'on-demand' intervention" — frame the structural difference between availability and dosage, without naming vendors. Use NSSA's HIT criteria as the evidence backbone.
topics.roman_take: The conviction angle — districts bought a vending machine when they needed a relationship; the data was always going to catch up; this is what happens when education procurement gets seduced by tech metrics (utilization, sessions logged) instead of outcome metrics (reclassification, growth).
topics.danielle_take: The implementation angle — what she actually sees when a charter switches from an on-demand vendor to A+: the first 60 days are about students learning the tutor's name, schedule, and expectations. Dosage without consistency is just data. ---
